_source.description: Placement rules in the advisor's per-device + between_topologies format. Bounding-rectangle model: each device is a 2D envelope; spacing values describe how envelopes relate along two perpendicular axes.

_directional_convention.active: spacing along the source-drain axis (the device's intrinsic 'active direction', NOT absolute X)
_directional_convention.poly: spacing along the channel-width axis (the device's intrinsic 'poly direction', NOT absolute Y)
_directional_convention.WPE_active / WPE_poly: Well-Proximity-Effect ordinal cost in the device's active and poly directions respectively. Layer-named (not axis-named) so the values stay correct when the placer rotates devices.
_directional_convention.negative_values: envelope overlap allowed in that axis — devices share the structure (merged diffusion, etc.)

device_constraints._comment: Manufacturing limits per device. Sizing only.

physical_design_rules._comment: Transistor-level layout rules from gpdk090 DRM. Used by block generator to compute finger dimensions and block sizes.

device_spacing._comment: Per-device rules for pairs of the SAME cell type. Negative active = envelope overlap allowed (shared diffusion). Categories follow the advisor's tree: common_bulk (shared bulk only), common_active (shared diffusion), same_cellNames (same cell, no sharing).

between_topologies._comment: Topology-distance costs for cross-device pairs (decision-tree leaves). active/poly leaves carry ordinal costs; bulk/group mismatch leaves use WPE-based costs because well-proximity is the dominant electrical effect when devices cross bulk or voltage-class boundaries.
